questions.question: Why did the Industrial Revolution begin in Britain?

options.A: Availability of colonies and raw materials

questions.explanation: Britain had colonies, capital, raw materials, and political stability.

questions.question: How did the invention of the steam engine transform industries?

options.B: Made factories independent of water sources

questions.explanation: Steam engines allowed factories to be set up anywhere.

questions.question: Why were Indian handloom weavers unable to compete with British goods?

options.B: High tariffs on Indian exports and cheap British imports

questions.question: What was the role of gomasthas in Indian villages?

options.B: Supervise and control weavers for the Company

questions.explanation: They ensured weavers sold only to the Company.

questions.question: How did railways contribute to industrial growth in India?

options.B: Connected raw material areas to ports and markets

questions.question: Why did industrialists use advertisements in colonial India?

options.B: To create demand and brand identity

questions.question: What led to the decline of Indian handicraft exports in the 19th century?

options.B: Loss of traditional markets and British tariff policies

questions.explanation: Indian goods faced heavy duties in Britain.

questions.question: Why were jobbers important in Indian factories?
questions.explanation: Jobbers hired workers and maintained discipline.

questions.question: How did World War I affect Indian industries?

options.B: Boosted production due to reduced imports

questions.explanation: Indian industries expanded to meet wartime demand.

questions.question: Why did hand production not disappear completely?

options.B: Handicrafts met niche and luxury demand

questions.question: Which factor supported the growth of Indian entrepreneurs in the early 20th century?
How did industrial cities impact workers’ lives?
What was the impact of machine-made yarn on Indian weavers?
Why did Indian industrialists support the nationalist movement?

options.B: To seek protection from foreign competition

questions.explanation: They wanted tariff protection from British imports.

questions.question: What was the main objective of the Swadeshi Movement?
Which sector showed significant expansion during the interwar period in India?
How did colonial policies restrict Indian industrial growth?
Why did peasants migrate to cities?
How did factory production change work discipline?
questions.explanation: Workers had to follow strict schedules.

questions.question: Which factor encouraged Indian mill growth after 1919?
questions.explanation: Government imposed tariffs to protect industries.

questions.question: Why did traditional artisans face unemployment?
questions.explanation: Factory goods were cheaper and mass-produced.

questions.question: How did credit networks support Indian industries?
What was the social impact of industrialization?

options.B: Class divisions between owners and workers

questions.question: Why did some Indian mills survive during economic crises?
Which concept best describes the decline of handicrafts due to British industrial imports?
questions.explanation: Deindustrialization refers to decline of traditional industries.
